title: Graellsia integrifolia
intro: Graellsia integrifolia, also known as Wild Valerian, is a perennial plant belonging to the Valerianaceae family. It is valued for its potential health benefits, including sedative and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate anxiety, insomnia, and muscle pain. The bioactive constituents of Graellsia integrifolia include valerenic acid, valepotriates, and isovaltrate, among others, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. The plant is commonly used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, often used as a natural remedy for promoting relaxation and sleep.
taxonomy: Graellsia integrifolia is a species of fungus. It belongs to the phylum Ascomycota. Within the phylum Ascomycota, Graellsia integrifolia is classified as a member of the class Pezizomycetes. The class Pezizomycetes is further divided into the order Pezizales, and Graellsia integrifolia specifically falls within this order. This classification places Graellsia integrifolia among other fungi that resemble cups or saucers in shape.
